headline: Oil posts largest quarterly price drop in 6 years
detail: Easing energy costs reduce input inflation broadly, which gives the Fed more room and keeps growth/tech valuations supported. For your chip and tech-heavy book, lower inflation expectations are a quiet tailwind - less pressure on rates means less pressure on high-multiple names like CRWD and the QQQ/SMH complex.
rec: No specific trade triggered by this alone, but it reinforces holding your current tech positions rather than trimming - macro backdrop just got a little friendlier for your book.

headline: Anthropic launches AI drug discovery program
detail: Another major AI lab expanding into a high-value vertical keeps the AI spending narrative strong. This matters for your book because sustained AI capex demand is the core thesis behind NVDA, SMH, and SOXL. No single-stock impact directly, but it counters any 'AI hype is fading' narrative.
rec: No new trade warranted from this headline alone - it's supportive color for positions you already hold, not a new entry signal. Hold SMH and SOXL and let the thesis play out.

headline: Nike tops estimates despite 12% China sales drop
detail: Tariff refund of nearly $1 billion for Nike signals that tariff relief mechanisms are real and being applied - this is a mild positive for any tech/chip name exposed to trade policy risk. It also shows the market is rewarding companies that beat despite macro headwinds, which is a healthy backdrop.
rec: No direct holding impact - you own no retail names. File it as a data point that tariff risk may be moderating, which is quietly supportive of your chip names but not a reason to add today.

headline: Bitcoin ETF selloff resilience being tested
detail: Crypto volatility is elevated and Bitcoin ETF flows are under pressure - this matters only tangentially for your book, but risk-off moves in crypto can occasionally bleed into high-beta tech sentiment. CRWD and leveraged names like SOXL and SPAL could see sympathy selling if crypto stress escalates.
rec: No action now, but watch CRWD (+6.07%) - if crypto-driven risk-off picks up intraday, high-beta growth names get hit first; your $590 stop on CRWD is far away, so mentally note the level and don't let a winner turn into a problem on margin.
